title: 7 Mistakes to Avoid When Buying Your First Home
content:
Critical errors first-time homebuyers should avoid.

1. SKIPPING PRE-APPROVAL:
Getting pre-approved ensures you understand your budget, strengthens your offer, and prevents surprises. Signals to sellers you're qualified and serious.

2. FOCUSING ONLY ON THE HOME, NOT THE LOCATION:
Properties can be renovated, neighborhoods cannot. Evaluate school districts, commute times, amenities, and development plans. Visit areas at different times of day.

3. DRAINING SAVINGS FOR THE DOWN PAYMENT:
Preserve emergency funds. Homeownership involves unexpected expenses for repairs, maintenance, and moving costs. Don't put every dollar toward down payment.

4. MAKING BIG PURCHASES BEFORE CLOSING:
Avoid opening new credit cards, financing furniture, or making large purchases after loan application submission. Lenders monitor financial stability throughout approval period.

5. WAIVING INSPECTIONS:
Professional inspections uncover hidden issues untrained eyes miss. Don't forego this protection even in competitive markets.

6. LETTING EMOTIONS DRIVE THE DECISION:
Balance enthusiasm with practicality. Evaluate whether the property genuinely supports your lifestyle and long-term goals.

7. NOT ASKING ENOUGH QUESTIONS:
Inquire about system ages, HOA fees, utility costs, resale potential, and other specifics for a smoother transition.
